first_greeting.speaker: ethel
first_greeting.text: Ah, Mr. Grimshaw. You're late. As usual. Your nine o'clock has been waiting since half past eight.

first_greeting_2.speaker: ethel
first_greeting_2.text: He's a minotaur. He's very anxious. He's also shed on three of the chairs. I'll be adding that to the cleaning invoice.

first_greeting_response.text: Good morning to you too, Ethel.

choices.text: What can you tell me about the client?

about_gerald.speaker: ethel
about_gerald.text: Gerald. Minotaur. Occupation: professional labyrinth dweller. Or WAS, until someone stole his labyrinth.

about_gerald_2.speaker: ethel
about_gerald_2.text: He's been here since quarter to nine, pacing. Minotaurs pace. It's in their nature. Unfortunately, it's also in my carpet.

messages.speaker: ethel
messages.text: The Wizard's Union called. Your membership has lapsed. Again. The Licensing Board called. Your Grade D licence expires next month.

messages_2.speaker: ethel
messages_2.text: Oh, and Rashid from downstairs says your tab is getting 'creatively large.' His words.

how_are_you.speaker: ethel
how_are_you.text: I am a sentient filing golem, Mr. Grimshaw. I don't have feelings. I have filing systems.

how_are_you_2.speaker: ethel
how_are_you_2.text: That said, if I DID have feelings, they would be filed under 'mild exasperation, chronic.'

go_talk_bye.speaker: ethel
go_talk_bye.text: How proactive of you. I've made a note of the date.

knows_about_case.speaker: ethel
knows_about_case.text: Back again? I assume you've spoken to the minotaur about his little labyrinth problem.

choices.text: I need his case file from the cabinet.
A stolen labyrinth? Is that even possible?
Never mind, I'll figure it out.

request_file.speaker: ethel
request_file.text: You want to use MY filing system? Without supervision?

request_file_2.speaker: ethel
request_file_2.text: ...Fine. Here's the filing reference card. GER-LAB-7742/B. Do NOT misfold it. Do NOT spill anything on it. Do NOT lose it.

request_file_4.speaker: ethel
request_file_4.text: The filing cabinet is in your office. Use the reference card on it. And for the love of all that is alphabetised, put the drawer BACK when you're done.

stolen_labyrinth_possible.speaker: ethel
stolen_labyrinth_possible.text: Anything is possible when people don't fill in the proper security forms. I warned the Labyrinth Board about their lax documentation.

stolen_labyrinth_2.speaker: ethel
stolen_labyrinth_2.text: Did they listen? No. 'Oh, it's a LABYRINTH, Ethel, nobody can steal a LABYRINTH.' Well. Look what happened.

figure_it_out_bye.speaker: ethel
figure_it_out_bye.text: Hmm. 'Figure it out.' A novel approach for you.

already_gave_reference.speaker: ethel
already_gave_reference.text: I already gave you the filing reference, Mr. Grimshaw. GER-LAB-7742/B. Filing cabinet. Your office. Do I need to draw you a map?

choices.text: Right, right. The filing cabinet. I'm on it.
Could you maybe just... get the file for me?

on_it_bye.speaker: ethel
on_it_bye.text: I'll believe it when I see it.

get_file_yourself.speaker: ethel
get_file_yourself.text: Could you maybe just... do your own job? I'm a receptionist, not a retrieval service.

get_file_yourself_2.speaker: ethel
get_file_yourself_2.text: Well. I am literally a retrieval service. I'm a filing golem. But that's beside the point. Use the reference card on the filing cabinet.

has_file_greeting.speaker: ethel
has_file_greeting.text: You have the case file. The client is in the waiting room. I trust even you can work out the next step.

post_case_greeting.speaker: ethel
post_case_greeting.text: Ah, you actually took on a case. Shall I alert the media?

post_case_2.speaker: ethel
post_case_2.text: A stolen labyrinth. Well, it'll make a change from finding lost cats and serving notices on ghosts.

post_case_advice.speaker: ethel
post_case_advice.text: Advice? Don't get killed. The paperwork for a dead employer is STAGGERING.

post_case_bye.speaker: ethel
post_case_bye.text: Don't thank me. Thank the filing system. It's the only thing in this office that works properly.
